DESIGNING STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE IN BEAUTY SALON USING
KNOWLEDGE CONVERSION MODEL
The Case of Bunda Umar Muslimah Salon
LUTHFIANISA PUTRI KARLINA
NIM: 29119054
Date of Passing of Final Test (.../.../2021)
Date of Graduation Ceremony (.../.../2021)
Master’s Programme, Bandung Institute of Technology, 2021
Thesis Counsellor: Ir. C.O. Budi Permadi Iskandar, MSP
ABSTRACT
Bunda Umar Salon is a salon for Muslim women that offers a complete top to toe beauty services.
Bunda Umar Salon is always committed to providing quality service to its customers. However,
some customers still complain about the disappointing service provided by Bunda Umar Salon
employees.
The absence of written SOPs in operational activities has resulted in the Bunda Umar Salon being
unable to meet acceptable service quality standards. To produce satisfying services for customers,
a salon needs to have the right standard operating procedures. This study aims to design SOPs
following the circumstances of Bunda Umar Salon and its implementation plan. This research used
observations and literature reviews to determine the most appropriate method in designing Bunda
Umar Salon's SOPs.
The SOPs design begins with determining the service critical points in order to define the priority
SOPs. After that, a knowledge conversion process is carried out based on Nonaka's SECI Model,
a well-known knowledge conversion theory. Knowledge conversion is carried out to convert tacit
knowledge, an unwritten understanding of how to perform service procedures owned by all Bunda
Umar Salon employees, into an explicit knowledge form. The acquired explicit knowledge is then
compiled into a written SOP format and then tested in the field. The SOP trial process was carried
out to find out feedback from employees and customers. The contents of the SOP were then revised
based on those feedbacks. The SOP implementation plan is made with a strategy to address
employee resistance to change.
The SOPs, which contain the fixed procedures best practice for Bunda Umar Salon's services, have
proven to bring about a positive change. SOPs make it easier for employees to understand the flow
of work and increase their discipline at work. Employees who work according to the SOPs provide
satisfying service for customers. The SOPs also indirectly affects the increase of Bunda Umar
Salon revenue.
Keywords: beauty salon service, employee resistance to change, knowledge conversion,
NONAKA’s SECI model, standard operating procedure (SOP).

PERANCANGAN STANDAR OPERASIONAL PROSEDUR SALON KECANTIKAN
MENGGUNAKAN MODEL KONVERSI PENGETAHUAN
Studi Kasus Bunda Umar Salon Muslimah
LUTHFIANISA PUTRI KARLINA
NIM: 29119054
Tanggal Kelulusan Sidang (.../.../2021)
Tanggal Wisuda (.../.../2021)
Program Magister, Institut Teknologi Bandung, 2021
Pembimbing Thesis: Ir. C.O. Budi Permadi Iskandar, MSP
ABSTRAK
Salon Bunda Umar adalah salon khusus wanita muslim yang menawarkan pelayanan kecantikan
lengkap dari ujung kepala sampai ujung kaki. Salon Bunda Umar selalu berkomitmen untuk
memberikan pelayanan yang berkualitas bagi para pelanggannya. Namun sayangnya, masih ada
beberapa pelanggan yang mengeluhkan pelayanan mengecewakan dari karyawan Bunda Umar
Salon.
Tidak adanya standar operasional prosedur (SOP) tertulis menjadi penyebab utama Salon Bunda
Umar tidak dapat memberikan standar pelayanan yang berkualitas. Padahal untuk memberikan
pelayanan yang memuaskan pelanggan, penting bagi sebuah salon kecantikan untuk memiliki SOP
yang benar. Penelitian ini bertujuan untuk merancang SOP yang sesuai dengan keadaaan Salon
Bunda Umar dan rencana implementasinya. Metode penelitian yang dilakukan adalah dengan
melakukan observasi dan studi Pustaka untuk menentukan metode perancangan SOP Salon Bunda
Umar yang paling tepat.
Proses perancangan SOP Bunda Umar Salon diawali dengan menentukan titik pelayanan kritis
untuk menetukan prosedur layanan yang menjadi prioritas pembuatan SOP. Setelah itu dilakukan
proses konversi pengetahuan beradarkan Model SECI Nonaka, sebuah teori konversi pengetahuan
yang terkenal. Proses konversi pengetahuan dilakukan untuk mengubah pengetahuan tacit, yaitu
pemahaman tidak tertulis tentang cara melakukan prosedur pelayanan yang telah dimiliki oleh
seluruh karyawan Salon Bunda Umar, menjadi bentuk pengetahuan eksplisit. Pengetahuan
eksplisit yang didapat lalu disusun ke dalam format SOP tertulis untuk kemudian diuji coba di
lapangan. Proses uji coba SOP dilakukan untuk mengetahui tanggapan dari karyawan dan
pelanggan. Isi SOP kemudian direvisi berdasarkan tanggapan tersebut. Rencana implementasi
pelaksanaan SOP dibuat dengan strategi untuk mereduksi resistensi karyawan terhadap perubahan.
SOP yang berisi prosedur tetap terbaik bagi pelayanan Salon Bunda Umar terbukti telah membawa
perubahan yang positif. SOP tertulis mempermudah para karyawan untuk secara lebih jelas
memahami alur pekerjaan yang harus dilakukan dan meningkatkan kedisplinan mereka. Karyawan
yang bekerja sesuai SOP akan memberikan pelayanan yang memuaskan bagi pelanggan.
Pelaksanaan SOP juga secara tidak langsung berpengaruh terhadap peningkatan pendapatan Bunda
Umar Salon.
Keywords: jasa salon kecantikan, konversi pengetahuan, model SECI Nonaka, standar
operasional prosedur (SOP), resistensi karyawan terhadap perubahan.

CHAPTER 1 INTRODUCTION
1.1 Background
Caring for self-beauty in a beauty salon has become necessary for most women to improve
their self-esthetics or relax their bodies after having a busy day. One of factor determines a woman
in choosing a beauty salon is the salon's quality of service. Salons that provide satisfying beauty
services will be the customer’s top choice.
To produce satisfying services for customers, a salon needs to have the right standard
operating procedures. Standard operating procedures are written documents that contain a
collection of standardize techniques for carrying out an activity based on chronological order. For
companies engaged in services, SOPs are made primarily to establish the standardization of
services provided to customers.
All employees who work in a company need to make SOP their main guideline when doing
the ordered work. At Bunda Umar Salon - a salon specifically for Muslim women, which is the
main object of this research, all employees need to follow the company's standard procedures when
providing beauty services to customers. However, during its ten years of existence, Bunda Umar
Salon never had a written SOP. Beauty treatments carried out by employees are only based on
standard procedures which were conveyed verbally to them during the initial training.
The absence of written SOPs in operational activities makes it difficult for Bunda Umar
Salon management to control the standard service quality. Customers who feel that they receive
substandard service will express their disappointment in customer complaints—besides, PT.
Bunda Umar Sharia Group as the owner of Bunda Umar Salon, has plans to open several more
branches in other places, but the absence of SOPs as the quality control essential tool is an obstacle
to the plan. Therefore, Bunda Umar Salon's management needs to design the SOP that all
employees must perform to enforce quality service standards and a system that can make Bunda
Umar Salon operations' quality feasible to operate on a more extensive business scale.
1.2 Company Profile
Bunda Umar Salon is a salon specifically for Muslim woman located in Intan Business
Center (IBC) Shopping Block No. A25, Garut Regency, West Java. Established on March 14th,

2010, Bunda Umar is the first Muslimah salon in Garut. Bunda Umar uses Islamic sharia as its

Figure 1.1. Bunda Umar Salon Logo (Author, 2021)
Bunda Umar Salon targets Muslim women as their main potential customers. Bunda Umar
Salon strives to give extra comfort and privacy, especially for Muslim women. The salon is
restricted to women only which means no men can enter the salon during operating hours. Bunda
Umar Salon only uses halal products for the service and doesn't provide beauty services against
Islamic sharia, such as hair extension, waxing, and eyebrow embroidery.
On the first of its operation, Bunda Umar runs only by five therapists, but now there are
twenty therapists with different specialty skills. Bunda Umar Salon building consists of 3 stories.
In the 1st floor, there are a receptionist room, lobby, and hair therapy room. The 2nd floor has a
facial treatment room containing four facial beds and two spa rooms. In the 3rd floor, there are a
menipedicure room, two spa rooms, a sauna room, and a praying room.
1.2.1 Vision and Mission
The vision of Bunda Umar Salon is to be the most extensive Muslimah-only salon brand in
Indonesia that provides professional services with premium quality by upholding Islamic Sharia's
value as its core business foundation.
The mission of Bunda Umar Salon are :
• Providing a comfortable and stimulating atmosphere where customers will be able to
relax, both their bodies and minds, and reconnect with their utmost goals amid their
daily lives, through experience various services - from hair, body, to facial treatments
• Promoting Islamic values as life guidance for Muslimah in terms of beauty and health
• Encouraging and facilitating women to have productive expertise, as a support for
women empowerment movement in terms of work accessibility, welfare, and skills.

1.2.2 Business Scope
Salon Bunda Umar tries to reach all Muslim women living in the Garut Regency area.
Since it is located in the middle of the Garut Kota area, customers who come are dominated by
women who live in Garut Kota District and the closest sub-districts around it, namely Tarogong
Kidul, Tarogong Kaler, Karangpawitan, Wanaraja, and Bayongbong Districts.
1.2.3 Company Product
Bunda Umar Salon service is relatively complete from hair to toe. Below is a menu of
treatments offered by Bunda Umar Salon.
a. Hair Treatment: hair cutting, hair wash, hair straightening, blow dry, hair mask, cream
bath, hair treatment, hair spa, halal hair colouring, and hair smoothing.
b. Face Treatment: facial, facial acupressure, and eye treatment.
c. Body Treatment: body spa, body massage, scrubbing, ratus, manicure, and pedicure.
d. Others: Pre-marital treatment

responsible for room cleanliness and treatment equipment condition on each floor.
f. Warehouse Chief
A warehouse chief responsible for managing inventory in-and-out activities, ordering
new supplies, and maintaining suitable inventory levels.

1.2.5 Business Process
Figure 1.6. Bunda Umar Salon Service Process (Author, 2021)
The service begins with a reservation process, which allows customers to book an
appointment prior to their arrival. This could be done by contacting Bunda Umar Salon service via
WhatsApp chat. The reservation could prevent customers from waiting too long in the queue. Then
when customers arrive, they need to select the menu provided in the menu book placed on the
reception table. After that, they have to wait 5-10 minutes until their therapist picks them up to the
treatment room. The treatment process duration varies from 30 - 120 minutes for every treatment.
After the treatment finishes, customers go to the cashier table to pay for the bill.
1.3 Business Issues
Having 3 branches in the next 5 years is one of the Bunda Umar Salon’s KPI (key
performance indicator) created by management. Therefore, PT. BUSG needs to ensure Bunda
Umar Salon operations' quality is feasible to operate on a more extensive business scale. Moreover,
in the future, the emergence of new competitors in the same field can be a severe threat to Bunda
Umar Salon's existence. Bunda Umar Salon, currently the only salon specifically for Muslim
women in Garut Regency, has the opportunity to become a top of mind woman salon if it always
maintains its service quality.
Keeping the customer satisfaction rate above 95% is also the Bunda Umar's Salon services
KPI. Providing high-quality services can lead to customer satisfaction. Quality service depends on
how all the Bunda Umar Salon employees can carry out beauty treatment procedures according to
company standards.

CHAPTER 2 BUSINESS ISSUE EXPLORATION
2.1 Literature Review
2.1.1 Standard Operating Procedure
Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is a written document that regulates the standardized
stages of a permanent, routine, and unchanging work procedure (Budiharjo, 2014). According to
United States Environmental Protection Agency (2007), the development and use of SOPs are an
integral part of a successful quality system as it provides individuals with the information to
perform a job properly and facilitates consistency in the quality and integrity of a product or end-
result.
Supartono (2016) explained that the SOP-making cycle consists of several processes
described as follows. The first process is writing SOPs. Writing SOPs begins by listing all activities
that must be carried out to meet the organization's service standards. SOP writing can be done
individually or in groups. Each organization has a unique character under the institutional
conditions and resources. Therefore, writing SOPs must be specified following the organization's
characteristics in which the SOP is implemented, but still follow a specific format. There are many
SOP writing format choices; every organization needs to choose one easy, concise format but
quality.
The second process is the SOP review. In this process, the written SOP needs to be
reviewed by the reviewer, who is assigned to assess the SOP's feasibility. Reviewers can come
from within or outside the organization. The assessment can be carried out in a meeting forum by
inviting the SOP compilers and the reviewers. SOP feasibility assessment focuses on three
essential things, namely the format, language, and content of the SOP. After completing the review
and correcting any possible errors, then the draft SOP is tested.
The trial process aims to ensure that the SOPs created can be implemented safely,
comfortably, and produce quality services or products. SOP trial activities are carried out similarly
to actual service activities but differ in the subject matter. The service subject is voluntary and not
yet destined for public service. Pilot activities should be carried out under expert supervision.
Record the results in the minutes of the trial SOP draft. If the trial succeeds in providing quality,
safe, and comfortable results, the SOP draft can be approved as an SOP.

The next process is the approval or authorization. Authorization is the SOP draft's approval
to become an SOP as a service standard, evidenced by the issuance of a decision letter by the
applicable administrative provisions. Authorization is carried out by officials who have the
authority and responsibility in the organization. After authorization, it then goes into the
determination process.
The process of establishing SOPs aims to provide a legal umbrella for SOPs to be
implemented in public service activities. Implementers and service recipients are obliged to follow
the stipulated SOP.
The SOP that has been determined must then be documented under the applicable
regulations. The originals are stored in the archive unit in hardware and software, three copies
each. Original documents must be properly maintained and periodically checked for integrity.
Original documents can be reproduced as needed and stored in the integral unit.
The next process is the publication, but this process is only mandatory for state public
service providers. In this process, the SOP document must be converted into public material.
Several forms of public material that must be created include public articles, brochures, posters,
books, pocketbooks, standing banners, running text, etc.
The established SOPs then enter the socialization process to administrative staff to be
familiar with, understand, and follow the applicable regulations therein. This socialization is
conveyed directly in various forums or means in the organization, for example, morning reports,
weekly reports, organizational meetings, or events. SOP socialization in general, while specific
delivery is carried out through training activities.
Implementers must attend SOP-based training, which means using SOPs as the primary
training material. In the training process, SOPs need to be explained, understood, and trained to
implement staff to understand the service's essential things. Besides, in this training, the
participants will practice (simulate) work on services according to the SOP, with the aim that later
they can work steadily (firmly) without feeling awkward, afraid of being wrong, and not
endangering service recipients. Through this training, the staff will work according to the SOP and
provide the best service for the community. This training will strengthen the organization's system
and work culture to work based on a system not based on individuals (personal opinion). Finally,
organizational services will take place in a systemic, sustainable, and "eternal" manner.

2.1.2 Knowledge Conversion
Knowledge as a resource provides a competitive edge to firms and ensures organizational
success (Kainth & Vij, 2017). According to Nonaka (1994), organizational knowledge is created
through a continuous dialogue between tacit and explicit knowledge. Tacit knowledge is
component of knowledge that is widely held by individuals but not able to be readily expressed. It
is expertise, skill, and ‘know how’, as opposed to codified knowledge (Dampney et al., 2002). The
tacit knowledge can be gained mostly from experiences, from other people, as for instance, riding
a bike or speaking a language (Keglovits, 2013).
The concept of “tacit knowledge” is a cornerstone in organizational knowledge creation
theory and covers knowledge that is unarticulated and tied to the senses, movement skills, physical
experiences, intuition, or implicit rules of thumb. Tacit knowledge differs from “explicit

Explicit knowledge can be expressed in words and numbers and shared in kind of knowledge can
be readily transmitted between individuals formally and systematically (Nonaka & Konno, 1998).
The concept of “knowledge conversion” explains how tacit and explicit knowledge interact
along a continuum (Nonaka & von Krogh, 2009). The interactions between these kinds of
knowledge lead to the creation of new knowledge (Nonaka & Konno, 1998). This research's
knowledge conversion method refers to Nonaka's SECI model, a well-known technique to convert
tacit knowledge into explicit knowledge.
2.1.2.1 Nonaka’s SECI Model
In Nonaka’s knowledge spiral theory, four basic patterns for creating knowledge in any
organization are suggested: socialization, externalization, combination and internalization (Huang
& Wang, 2002). The SECI model escribes a dynamic process in which explicit and tacit knowledge
are exchanged and transformed (Nonaka & Konno, 1998). Nonaka's SECI can be described as

Socialization. Socialization involves the sharing of tacit knowledge between individuals
through physical proximity. Tacit knowledge is exchanged through joint activities – such as being
together, spending time, living in the same environment – rather than through written or verbal
instructions. The process of transferring one’s ideas or images directly to colleagues or
subordinates means to share personal knowledge and create a common place.
Externalization. Externalization requires the expression of tacit knowledge and its
translation into comprehensible forms that others can understand. The unspoken understanding is
articulated into explicit knowledge form by involving techniques that help express one's ideas or
images as words, concepts, figurative language, and visuals. In this process, the tacit knowledge
of experts is translated into a readily understandable form.
Combination. Combination involves the conversion of explicit knowledge into more
complex sets of explicit knowledge. The key issues are communication and diffusion process and
the systemization of knowledge. The combination phase relies on three approaches:
• capturing and integrating new explicit knowledge
• transferring the knowledge directly to disseminate the explicit knowledge
• editing or processing the explicit knowledge into more usable documents (e.g.,
plans, reports, data).
Internalization. The internalization of newly created knowledge is the conversion of
explicit knowledge into the organization's tacit knowledge. The explicit knowledge has to be
embodied in all the people's actions and practices in the organization.
(Nonaka & Konno, 1998)
2.1.3 Employee Resistance to Change
In this modern era, everything will change because change is everywhere, including in an
organization (Darmawan & Azizah, 2019). Changes are also mainly made by business
organizations, which are always struggling with intense competition. Every small or medium
business must be able to make changes quickly to maintain its competitiveness since the core
competitiveness is the capability of a company to survive while facing intense competition (Yuh-
Shy, 1999).
Not everyone gives the same reaction to a change, in this case, employees' reaction to their
company organizational changes. In the face of change, some people will accept, while some

specific change and individual conditions and perceptions. Furthermore, acceptance or rejection
often depends on the personal loss or gain that will result from the change in the tangible areas of,
for example, money, visibility, working conditions, authority, or responsibility, as well as from

Employee resistance to change is employee’s negative attitude or counterproductive

described resistance to change is often cited as a reason for difficulties in implementing and the
failure of change initiatives. Employees who were inclined to resist changes were more distraught
by the change and reported an increased difficulty to work effectively (Oreg, 2003). People resist
change because changes are seen as a threat to them, their status and financial rewards. Resistance
to change is considered to be one of the key factors in failing on the success of changes in business
(Karaxha, 2019).
2.2 Conceptual Framework
Conceptual framework refers to specific ideas a researcher utilizes in his/her study based
on the concepts, which are the main study variables. It can also be described as an adaptation of a
model in an existing theory that a researcher adapts to suit the research purpose. The aim of making
a conceptual framework is to make research findings more meaningful, acceptable to the
theoretical construct in the research field, and ensures generalizability (Adom et al., 2018).
This research begins with a discussion of management issues as the primary research
object. Chapter 1 revealed that the absence of written SOPs was the root cause of the Bunda Umar
Salon service quality problems, so that designing the SOP and its implementation plan became the
main objectives of this study.
SOPs are written documents that contain instructions on how to carry out a procedure
correctly. In order to determine the instructions that produce the correct procedure, knowledge is
needed. The Concise Oxford Dictionary (1998) defines knowledge as awareness or familiarity
gained by experience (of a person, fact, or thing). Therefore, the knowledge possessed by the
owner and all employees of Bunda Umar Salon is the primary basis for making the required SOPs.
Based on this research context, there is a relationship between SOPs and the knowledge
creation process. Nonaka and Takeuchi (1995) in Chou and Tsai (2003) described that the key to

2.3 Research Methodology
This research uses descriptive exploratory qualitative research methods. According to
Arikunto (2002) in Mabrudy (2013), descriptive research analyzes and presents data systemically
so that it is easier to understand, while exploratory research is a type of research to find something
new in the form of grouping a fact. Descriptive exploratory research does not aim to test
hypotheses but provide a description of a phenomenon's state.
2.4 Data Collection Method
This study uses primary data and secondary data to collect all the necessary information.
Primary data were obtained by conducting observations and interviews, while secondary data were
obtained using literature reviews from internal and public data.
2.4.1 Primary Data
2.4.1.1 Observation
Observation is used in the social sciences to collect data about people, processes, and
cultures (Kawulich, 2012). A scientific observation method is the technique of watch, evaluate,
draw conclusions, and make comments on interactions and relations carried out systematically,
purposefully, and on scientific grounds (Ciesielska et al., 2018).
This research uses a non-participant observation method, which according to Ciesielska et
al. (2018), means that the researcher tries to understand the world, relationships, and interactions
in a new way, without prevalent categorizations and evaluations. Observations are made by
observing all service processes that occur during Bunda Umar Salon's operational hours and noting
important things related to standardization of procedures. The researcher also observed the
incoming customer complaint data as complementary information that could support the
observation process.
The aim of this process is to observe the salon services and note some of its critical points
that can lead to customer dissatisfaction issues. The observations can provide data that shows
which service sector is a priority for the SOP design.

CHAPTER 3 BUSINESS SOLUTION
3.1 Determine Service Critical Point
According to Supartono (2016), writing SOPs needs to be started by listing what activities
must be carried out to meet organizational service standards. According to Parasuraman et al.
(1985), management should understand which features a service must have to meet the customer
needs and what levels of those features perform to deliver high-quality service. Bunda Umar
Salon's owner thinks that her employees' service meets the service standard if there are no customer
complaints. Therefore, the researcher started the SOP design process by listing the service critical
point, which means what service activities were at risk of causing customer complaints. The
research uses a direct observation method to determine the critical point. Service critical points
obtained after the observation process are verified by comparing the observation results with
customer complaint data.
3.1.1 Service Critical Point in Lobby Area
Bunda Umar Salon lobby area includes a reception service area and a public waiting room,
which means that the customers and their companions can wait in this room. The observations
show that employee service at the reception is the first critical service point that determines
customer satisfaction. Here employees are required to understand and explain all the services
offered to customers. If the employee cannot provide the right treatment recommendations for the
customer's condition, the customer will not be satisfied with the services provided. Employees at
the reception desk must also understand and offer available promos or complement treatments that
match the customer's primary treatment to increase Bunda Umar Salon's sales.
The second critical point is when a customer has to wait, employees in the lobby area need
to explain how long customers should wait and, if possible, to explain the circumstances that make
the customer need to wait. Customers who wait too long for no apparent reason will feel angry
with the services.
3.1.2 Service Critical Point in Treatment Area
Bunda Umar Salon's treatment room includes all rooms used by the customer during the
treatment. Bunda Umar Salon has two types of treatment rooms, namely closed and open treatment
rooms. A closed treatment room is a private treatment room and is only used by one customer and
one therapist during the treatment. Included in the closed treatment room is the spa room and the

3.2.1 Socialization
Socialization is an activity to capture Bunda Umar Salon's service procedures knowledge,

3.2.2 Externalization
At the externalization stage, all tacit knowledge obtained from the socialization process is
transferred to an explicit form, namely by writing notes on carrying out an operation. The resulting
document is a simple record of the business process, becoming the primary data source for the
combination process.
3.2.3 Combination
In the combination stage, the business process documents that have been obtained
previously will be converted into more complex sets of explicit knowledge. The research will
combine business process documents with other explicit knowledge obtained from literature
reviews and benchmarking of competitor salon SOPs to get best practice service procedures
implemented at Bunda Umar Salon.

• Receiving Online Reservations
• Covid-19 Transmission Prevention Operational Protocol
• Beauty Treatment Procedures
3.3.1 SOP Writing
The initial stage of the SOP design process is writing the SOP into a specified format.
Wieringa et al. (1998), in Ambarwati & Triana (2012), states that there are four formats for writing
SOPs as described below.
• Simple steps. SOP writing format for routine activities that have short process steps
and do not require much decision making.
• Hierarchical steps. SOP writing format for routine activities with more than ten steps
of the procedure but does not require many decisions.
• Graphic steps. The definition is the same as hierarchical steps, but in this format, the
SOP is written in graphical form to simplify long processes into shorter forms.
• Flowchart. SOP writing format in the form of a simple graphic describes the process
steps in making a decision. The flowchart format is suitable for explaining procedures
that require a lot of decision making.
According to Stup (2012), management can choose various SOP writing formats, but they
need to choose a writing format that is easy for employees to understand to simplify the work. The
table below explains how Stup (2012) provides recommendations on choosing an SOP writing
format that suits the company's needs based on several criteria.

3.3.2 SOP Trial
At this stage, the researcher conducted a trial on the draft SOP. According to Supartono
(2016), SOPs trial is needed to ensure that SOPs can be implemented comfortably and produce
high-quality services. SOP trial is conducted by carrying out procedures similar to the actual
service activities in the actual room the service will be performed.
After completing the writing, the draft SOP was distributed to Bunda Umar Salon's
employees through a meeting. All employees were gathered, and the researchers, together with the
management staff, explained the contents of the draft SOP and gave orders for employees to carry
out the procedures in the SOP for the next two weeks as a form of SOP trial. Researchers made
observations during the SOP trial period to ensure the trial went according to plan.
The SOP draft trial must be able to answer several essential questions. If all the questions
are answered, the SOPs can be approved and implemented. The list of questions that need to be
answered is as follows.
• Does the capacity and resources of the organization enable the delivery of services as stated
in the SOP?
• Can the services contained in the SOP be carried out safely and comfortably?
• Can the staff of the organization carry it out quickly?
• Are the service (products) delivered in good quality?
(Supartono, 2016)

3.3.3 SOP Review
The researcher conducted interviews with eight employees and six customers each
separately to find out feedback on the SOP contents after the trial period was completed. Based on
the results of interviews with employees, they all thought that the procedural steps contained in
the SOP draft were easy to practice in the field because they were the same as their previous tacit
knowledge. They also said that written SOPs made their performance standardized, and they could
also remind each other if there were employees who did not provide services according to the
specified standards. Written SOPs indirectly increase their work discipline. Of all the procedures
in the draft SOP, five employees suggested that the standardized gestures and sentences when
greeting customers in the treatment room need to be adjusted according to the circumstances.
In the SOP draft, it is written:
• “Terapis sudah berdiri dengan seragam dan atribut lengkap. Sambil tersenyum
mengucap kalimat “Assalamualaikum, teteh/ibu (nama pelanggan), saya (nama
terapis), terapis yang akan menangangi hari ini””
• “Terapis yang menunggu di ruang perawatan wajib menyambut pelanggan yang
lewat di depan mereka. Sambutan dilakukan dengan berdiri, menyilangkan tangan
kanan ke atas, menaruh telapak tangan kanan di area dada atas kiri, tersenyum,
sedikit membungkukan badan atas, dan mengucapkan “Assalamualaikum, (Ibu/
Teteh)”. Setelah pelanggan berlalu, terapis dipersilakan duduk kembali.”
The employee requested that the underlined procedure be changed in terms of sentences and
movements to be more flexible to greet customers who have long been Bunda Umar Salon clients.
The draft SOP procedures make long-time customers awkward, and customers feel that employees
do not need to greet them too formally because they already know each other well.
In terms of practicing SOPs in the field during the trial period, from the results of the
interviews, it was found that some employees were still difficult to carry out procedures according
to the SOP, especially in the section on introducing themselves to customers and their attitudes
when waiting for treatment. Researchers received reports from respondents that their senior co-
workers often forgot to carry out guest welcoming procedures. The SOP also states a prohibition
for employees to wait on the stairs, but there are still some employees sitting there in the field.
This finding is evidence of employee resistance to change so that in preparing implementation
plans, researchers need to adopt strategies to reduce this resistance.

CHAPTER 4 CONCLUSION AND IMPLEMENTATION PLANS
4.1 Conclusion
Bunda Umar Salon, a salon for Muslim women that has been established in Garut since
2010, plans to expand its business scale by opening other branches in other new locations.
Unfortunately, there are still problems with the quality of service where there are no expected
quality and service standards in Bunda Umar Salon, and this problem leads to customer
dissatisfaction in the form of customer complaints.
The absence of written SOPs was the leading root cause of service quality problems. All
Bunda Umar Salon employees provide services based solely on their tacit knowledge of unwritten
service procedure. Therefore, the researchers conducted a study to find the best way to design the
SOP for Bunda Umar Salon.
Based on observations, interviews, and literature studies that have been carried out to
produce a good SOP design, the researcher formulates the solution stages as follows.
a. Determine service critical point
Service critical points are services that are vulnerable to customer complaints if
employees do not perform these services properly. The service critical points that are
found then become the basis for determining what services are the top priority for the
SOP.
b. Knowledge Conversion based on Nonaka’s SECI Model
The knowledge conversion process is carried out to convert tacit knowledge into
explicit knowledge. Researchers use Nonaka's SECI (Socialization, Externalization,
Combination, and Internalization) as the primary theoretical basis. The socialization
was carried out by holding discussions with the founder of Bunda Umar Salon and the
two most senior employees to collect tacit knowledge on service procedures. The tacit
knowledge recorded then goes through the externalization and combination process so
that it is finally ready to become material for the SOP design.
c. Design the SOPs
o SOP Writing
Bunda Umar Salon's SOPs are written in a simple step format because
almost all of the activities are short routine procedures without the need to make

enforcement implementation plan. The strategy to address employee resistance to change is
applied in the plan. The level and quality of communication about the change can affect resistance,
so it is essential to ensure that management and employees have a good communication
relationship. Erwin & Garman (2009) recommend the management strategy to address employee
resistance to change as follows.

4.2.1 Bunda Umar Salon SOPs Enforcement Plan
The main objective of the implementation plan is to ensure that the management can ensure
that all Bunda Umar Salon employees understand the contents of the SOP and carry out services
according to the fixed procedures listed in it. The followings are series of program included in
Bunda Umar Salon's SOP enforcement implementation plan proposed by the researcher. Each
program has the leading people in charge of (PIC) coming from the management staff of PT. Bunda
Umar Sharia Group to ensure the program runs well.
a. Affirmation of The Company’s Organizational Structure
A clear organizational structure is a primary basis for the appropriate
implementation of SOPs in a company. At Bunda Umar Salon, the existence of SOPs
has made several processes flow more formal for employees so that an exact mapping
of the people responsible for each procedure regulated in the SOP is required. According
to Ahmady et al. (2016), the organizational structure is a way or method by which
organizational activities are divided, organized, and coordinated. The organizations
created the structures to coordinate the activities of work factors and control the member
performance.

Suryaningsum (2008) also stated that organizational structure and supervision's
prominent roles are to coordinate employee activities, motivate employees, equip
employees with incentives, and change employees' behavior and value characteristics
as organizational members. Organizational structure can be a behavior controller since
it can shape the way people behave and determine how they act in an organization.
Therefore, the board of directors of PT Bunda Umar Sharia Group, as Bunda Umar
Salon's owner, needs to reaffirm the existing organizational structure followed by
preparing a job desk and clear lines of coordination between people in the organization
structure. The board of directors needs to ensure that the existing organizational
structure is according to explicit knowledge or documented SOPs. A good
organizational structure can encourage the SOP enforcement activity by creating clear
lines of communication and reporting.
b. SOP Retraining Program
This program aims to make employees better understand and remember all standard
procedures. The retraining program is carried out regularly. In the early days of
implementing SOPs, retraining needed to be held more often, but SOP retraining can
become an annual program if the employees are already used to it. The retraining
program begins with re-submitting material on implementing the SOP, which is
essential for all employees. This program can also allow employees and management
collaboration to formulate a better SOP implementation strategy. At the end of the
program, an exam or evaluation will be held to assess how well employees understand
and remember the SOP implementation. This program is the responsibility of the HRD
manager of PT. Bunda Umar Sharia Group.
c. SOP Enforcement Supervision
The enforcement of SOPs by all employees during operations must always be
supervised to ensure high-quality services. The manager who is responsible for
implementing this program is the operational manager of PT. Bunda Umar Sharia
Group. At the end of each month, the operational manager needs to report the SOP
enforcement. The enforcement report can be used as evaluation material for HRD
managers to improve employee performance, both in the next SOP retraining program
or monthly employee performance evaluation programs.

Employee reward programs need to be done regularly once a month to appreciate

4.3 Recommendation
The SOPs, which contain the best practice fixed procedures for Bunda Umar Salon's
services, has proven to bring about a positive change. SOPs make it easier for employees to
understand the flow of work and increase their discipline at work. Employees who work according
to the SOPs provide satisfying service for customers. This improvement indirectly also provides
more benefits for the company. Bunda Umar Salon's financial report shows that there has been an
increase in revenue in consecutive from October to December 2020, namely the month when
employees start working according to the SOPs. Therefore, management needs to continue to
complete SOPs for all service procedures performed at Bunda Umar Salon.
A complete SOP will make it easier for management, especially in training new employees
because, as previously stated, PT. Bunda Umar Sharia Group wants to increase the number of
Bunda Umar Salon branches in other places so that perhaps employees' recruitment and training
process will be carried out more frequently. The SOP that has been made must also be flexible to
change. Management needs to always make renewal efforts when necessary, for example, by
always doing benchmarking to similar industries, so that the services provided can continue to be
able to adapt to the dynamic trend changes.

Menerima Order Pelanggan di Meja Tgl. Revisi : -
Resepsionis Halaman : 1 dari 4
1. Tujuan
Memahami keinginan pelanggan, menawarkan service yang tepat, dan menindaklanjuti order perawatan
yang diinginkan pelanggan yang datang ke Salon.
2. Ruang Lingkup
Cara berkomunikasi dengan pelanggan ketika melakukan pemilihan menu perawatan di meja resepsionis.
3. Penanggung Jawab
Kepala salon dan manajer operasional.
4. Pelaksana
Kasir dan resepsionis
5. Alat dan Bahan
Buku menu, pulpen, dan formulir tindakan, bon order
6. Durasi Kegiatan
2-5 menit
7. Prosedur Tetap
7.1 Setelah melakukan SOP Menyambut Kedatangan Pelanggan, karyawan pelaksana mengarahkan
pelanggan ke depan meja resepsionis.
7.2 Karyawan pelaksana memperkenalkan diri dengan template dialog yang digunakan adalah :
“Selamat pagi/siang/sore, teteh/ibu. Dengan NAMA KARYAWAN PELAKSANA disini. Mau
perawatan apa hari ini?”
7.3 Setelah pelanggan mengucapkan jenis perawatan yang diinginkan, periksa ketersediaan terapis saat
itu dan konfirmasi estimasi waktu tunggu jika pelanggan harus mengantri.
7.4 Karyawan pelaksana menanyakan nama pelanggan dengan dialog : “Sebelumnya mohon maaf,
nama teteh/ibu siapa?”. Konfirmasi ulang nama pelanggan, jika sudah tepat selanjutnya selalu
gunakan nama panggilan pelanggan selama percakapan berlangsung.
7.5 Karyawan pelaksana memperlihatkan daftar menu perawatan kepada pelanggan.
7.6 Karyawan pelaksana menanyakan kondisi rambut/wajah/tubuh yang dimiliki oleh pelanggan terkait
dengan pemilihan menu perawatan.
7.7 Karyawan pelaksana memberikan rekomendasi menu perawatan yang sesuai dengan kondisi
pelanggan beserta penjelasan tahapan perawatannya.
7.8 Setelah pelanggan memilih perawatan yang diinginkan, karyawan pelaksana menentukan terapis
yang akan menangani pelanggan tersebut.
7.9 Karyawan pelaksana menawarkan promo yang sedang berlangsung, jika ada, dan tambahan menu
rekomendasi yang cocok dipadukan dengan perawatan yang dipilih pelanggan.
7.10 Karyawan mengonfirmasi ulang pesanan pelanggan dengan menyebutkan nama terapis, durasi, dan
biaya perawatan tersebut.

Halaman : 1 dari 1

Menunggu Tugas Penanganan
Halaman : 1 dari 1
1. Tujuan
Menjaga suasana salon agar tetap nyaman selama terapis belum mendapatkan pelanggan untuk dilayani
2. Ruang Lingkup
Cara terapis bersikap selama belum mendapatkan tugas penanganan.
3. Penanggung Jawab
Kepala salon dan manager operasional
4. Pelaksana
Terapis
5. Alat dan Bahan
Seragam dan atribut lengkap
6. Durasi Kegiatan : -
7. Prosedur Tetap
3.1 Terapis yang belum memiliki tugas, dipersilahkan untuk duduk di beberapa titik berikut dengan
kapasitas maksimal :
- Ruang tunggu perawatan lantai 1 = 3 orang terapis
- Ruang tunggu perawatan lantai 2 = 2 orang terapis
- Ruang tunggu perawatan lantai 3 = 2 orang terapis
- Ruang pantry terapis = tidak dibatasi
3.2 Selama menunggu di ruang tunggu perawatan, terapis harus duduk rapi, menggunakan seragam dan
atribut lengkap, dan tidak saling mengobrol dengan terapis lainnya.
3.3 Terapis tidak diperkenankan untuk makan, minum, dan melakukan panggilan telepon di ruang
tunggu perawatan. Kegiatan-kegiatan tersebut hanya dapat dilakukan di ruang pantry atau di luar
salon.
3.4 Terapis diperkenankan untuk memainkan gadget yang dimiliki, namun dalam mode tanpa suara.
3.5 “Terapis yang menunggu di ruang perawatan wajib menyadari keberadaan pelanggan yang lewat di
depan mereka dengan menunjukkan senyum sambil sedikit membungkukan badan tanda
penghormatan.”

Persiapan Station Rambut
Halaman : 1 dari 2
1. Tujuan
Menciptakan lingkungan kerja dan area perawatan rambut yang nyaman bagi pelanggan dan terapis.
2. Ruang Lingkup
Cara mempersiapkan station rambut sebelum dan setelah perawatan, checklist aspek yang harus dipenuhi,
dan kondisi yang dianggap sebagai kesalahan.
3. Penanggung Jawab
Kepala salon dan manager operasional
4. Pelaksana
Terapis
5. Alat dan Bahan
Alat kebersihan
6. Durasi Kegiatan : 5-10 menit
7. Prosedur Tetap
7.1. Unit yang termasuk ke dalam 1 station rambut adalah :
a) 1 buah kursi perawatan
b) 1 buah meja
c) 1 buah cermin
d) Permukaan lantai seluas area kerja setiap station
7.2. Persiapan station rambut dibagi ke dalam 2 kategori :
a) Persiapan sebelum perawatan, merupakan kegiatan pembersihan station sebelum
digunakan pelanggan
b) Pembersihan setelah perawatan, merupakan kegiatan pembersihan station setelah
digunakan untuk merawat pelanggan
7.3. Persiapan dan pembersihan dilakukan dengan :
a) Membersihkan seluruh permukaan cermin dengan lap dan cairan pembersih
b) Membersihkan seluruh permukaan meja dengan lap dan cairan pembersih
c) Membersihkan seluruh permukaan kursi dengan lap dan cairan pembersih
d) Menyapu lantai
e) Membersihkan permukaan lantai dengan pel dan cairan pembersih
7.4. Checklist aspek kesiapan station sebelum digunakan oleh pelanggan :
o Semua unit dalam station dalam keadaan kering
o Tidak ada noda di seluruh permukaan cermin, meja, dan kursi
o Tidak ada bekas rambut sehelai pun di seluruh bagian station
o Tidak ada bau yang mengganggu
o Posisi kursi menghadap ke cermin dengan jarak antara cermin dan sandaran kaki sebesar
lebar 1 kotak lantai.
o Lampu (bagian mana) dalam keadaan menyala.

text:
Jurnal Ekonomi, Manajemen, Bisnis dan Akuntansi Review Vol: 5, No 2, 2025, Page: 1-10
Evaluasi Kinerja Operasional Barber Shop Menggunakan
Pendekatan Supply Chain Management dan Manajemen
Produksi
Valentina Monoarfa, Siti Rahayu, Feibe Stevani Tandayu*, Idil, Moh Riyadh M. Noer
Universitas Negeri Gorontalo
Abstrak: Penelitian ini bertujuan untuk menilai kinerja operasional barbershop melalui penerapan pendekatan Supply
Chain Management (SCM) dan Manajemen Produksi. Sebagai unit usaha berbasis jasa, barbershop menuntut proses
operasional yang efektif untuk menjaga kepuasan pelanggan, mengurangi waktu tunggu, mengoptimalkan persediaan
bahan habis pakai, serta memastikan konsistensi layanan (Kotler & Keller, 2016). Analisis penelitian mencakup beberapa
elemen utama, antara lain manajemen pemasok, aliran persediaan, perancangan proses layanan, penjadwalan, serta
pengelolaan kapasitas. Hasil penelitian menunjukkan bahwa integrasi prinsip-prinsip SCM dengan perangkat manajemen
produksi mampu meningkatkan kualitas layanan, efisiensi operasional, dan pemanfaatan sumber daya secara signifikan
(Heizer, Render, & Munson, 2020). Rekomendasi yang diberikan meliputi penguatan hubungan dengan pemasok,
penerapan prosedur layanan yang terstandarisasi, pengembangan sistem penjadwalan yang lebih efektif, serta
pemanfaatan indikator kinerja untuk mendukung proses perbaikan berkelanjutan.
Kata Kunci: Barbershop, Evaluasi Kinerja, Supply Chain Management, Manajemen Produksi, Operasional Jasa, Efisiensi
Layanan.
DOI: Abstract: This study aims to assess the operational performance of a barbershop
https://doi.org/10.53697emba.v5i2.3389 business through the lenses of Supply Chain Management (SCM) and Production
*Correspondence: Feibe Stevani Tandayu Management. As a service-oriented enterprise, a barbershop requires highly efficient
Email: stevaniveibe@gmail.com
operational processes to ensure customer satisfaction, reduce waiting times, optimize
the inventory of consumable materials, and maintain consistency in service delivery
Received: 22-10-2025
(Kotler & Keller, 2016). The analysis focuses on several key dimensions, including
Accepted: 22-11-2025
Published: 22-12-2025 supplier coordination, inventory flow, service process design, scheduling, and capacity
management. The findings indicate that integrating SCM principles with production
management tools can substantially enhance service quality, operational efficiency, and
the effective utilization of resources (Heizer, Render, & Munson, 2020). Based on these

under the terms and conditions of the
performance indicators to support continuous improvement initiatives.
Creative Commons Attribution (CC BY)
license Keywords: Barbershop, Supply Chain Management, Production Management,
(http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/ Operational Performance, Service Efficiency.
4.0/).
Pendahuluan
Industri barbershop merupakan salah satu subsektor jasa yang mengalami
pertumbuhan signifikan dalam beberapa tahun terakhir. Peningkatan gaya hidup modern,
kesadaran masyarakat terhadap grooming, serta munculnya konsep barbershop
kontemporer yang mengedepankan kenyamanan, estetika, dan layanan personal
menjadikan sektor ini semakin kompetitif. Barbershop saat ini tidak hanya berfungsi
sebagai tempat pemotongan rambut, tetapi telah berkembang menjadi ruang layanan
https://penerbitadm.pubmedia.id/index.php/JER

Jurnal Ekonomi, Manajemen, Bisnis dan Akuntansi Review Vol: 5, No 2, 2025 2 of 10
profesional yang menekankan kualitas, kecepatan pelayanan, dan kepuasan pelanggan.
Kondisi tersebut menuntut pemilik usaha untuk mampu mengelola aspek operasional
secara efektif agar dapat mempertahankan kualitas layanan di tengah persaingan yang
semakin kompleks.
Secara operasional, barbershop memiliki karakteristik yang berbeda dibandingkan
bisnis jasa lainnya. Proses layanan yang relatif singkat, kebutuhan tenaga kerja yang
terampil, serta ketergantungan pada ketersediaan bahan habis pakai seperti neck paper,
alkohol, hand sanitizer, sampo, dan produk styling rambut, menuntut adanya sistem
manajemen operasional yang terstruktur. Tanpa pengelolaan yang memadai, berbagai
permasalahan operasional dapat muncul, antara lain antrean yang panjang, ketidaktepatan
durasi layanan, ketidakseragaman kualitas layanan antar barber, serta kekurangan stok
yang dapat berdampak langsung pada tingkat kepuasan pelanggan.
Di sisi lain, Supply Chain Management (SCM) juga memiliki peran penting dalam
menjaga kelancaran proses operasional barbershop. Meskipun merupakan bisnis berbasis
jasa, barbershop tetap mengandalkan rantai pasok barang fisik seperti peralatan grooming,
kosmetik rambut, kain pelindung, perlengkapan kebersihan, hingga perangkat listrik
seperti clipper dan trimmer. Ketidakefisienan dalam pengelolaan rantai pasok dapat
menimbulkan berbagai isu operasional, seperti ketidakstabilan suplai, peningkatan biaya
pengadaan, hingga terjadinya downtime layanan akibat kerusakan atau kekurangan
peralatan. Oleh karena itu, pengendalian supply chain menjadi elemen krusial dalam
mendukung keberlanjutan operasional.
Selain SCM, konsep manajemen produksi juga relevan diterapkan pada operasional
barbershop. Meskipun output utamanya berupa jasa, barbershop tetap memiliki alur proses
produksi layanan yang terdiri dari tahap kedatangan pelanggan, registrasi, konsultasi gaya
rambut, pelaksanaan pemotongan, hingga tahap finishing dan pembayaran. Setiap tahapan
memerlukan penerapan standar operasional prosedur (SOP) untuk memastikan kualitas
layanan yang konsisten. Manajemen produksi juga berperan dalam pengaturan kapasitas
layanan, penjadwalan barber, serta perbaikan alur kerja guna meminimalkan pemborosan
waktu, tenaga, dan biaya operasional.
Permasalahan tersebut menunjukkan perlunya integrasi antara SCM dan manajemen
produksi dalam mengevaluasi dan meningkatkan kinerja operasional barbershop.
Penerapan konsep SCM memungkinkan barbershop untuk mengelola rantai pasok secara
lebih efisien melalui pengendalian ketersediaan barang, optimalisasi hubungan dengan
pemasok, serta pengurangan biaya pengadaan. Sementara itu, manajemen produksi
berkontribusi terhadap pengembangan alur layanan, penerapan SOP yang seragam,
pengaturan kapasitas pelayanan, serta peningkatan produktivitas barber.
Dalam operasional barbershop, berbagai permasalahan sering muncul dan
berdampak langsung terhadap kelancaran pelayanan serta kepuasan pelanggan. Salah satu
kendala yang kerap terjadi adalah keterlambatan pengadaan bahan habis pakai, seperti
shaving gel, neck strip, tisu, dan bahan sanitasi. Kondisi ini membuat proses pelayanan
terganggu karena barber tidak dapat bekerja secara maksimal tanpa ketersediaan bahan
tersebut. Permasalahan ini diperparah dengan ketiadaan sistem kontrol persediaan yang
https://penerbitadm.pubmedia.id/index.php/JER

Jurnal Ekonomi, Manajemen, Bisnis dan Akuntansi Review Vol: 5, No 2, 2025 3 of 10
memadai. Tanpa pencatatan atau pemantauan stok yang teratur, barbershop sering
mengalami kekurangan bahan pada saat dibutuhkan atau malah menumpuk stok yang
tidak segera digunakan, sehingga menambah biaya penyimpanan dan potensi pemborosan.
Ketiadaan standar prosedur layanan (Standard Operating Procedure/SOP)
menyebabkan kualitas pelayanan menjadi tidak konsisten antar barber. Setiap barber
cenderung menggunakan cara sendiri, sehingga pengalaman yang diterima pelanggan
berbeda-beda dan sulit bagi manajemen untuk memastikan kualitas yang seragam. Di sisi
lain, penjadwalan barber yang kurang optimal juga menimbulkan masalah, terutama ketika
jumlah pelanggan meningkat pada jam tertentu. Tanpa sistem penjadwalan yang baik,
waktu tunggu pelanggan menjadi panjang dan berpotensi menurunkan kepuasan serta
mengurangi peluang repeat order.
Barbershop juga sering menghadapi tantangan terkait tidak adanya analisis beban
kerja. Ketika beban kerja barber tidak dihitung dengan tepat, terjadi ketidakseimbangan
antara jumlah tenaga kerja dan volume pelanggan. Akibatnya, produktivitas menurun
karena barber bekerja terlalu berat atau justru menganggur pada waktu tertentu. Kondisi
tersebut turut mendorong pemborosan biaya operasional. Biaya meningkat karena
pembelian bahan menjadi tidak terencana, terutama ketika pembelian dilakukan secara
mendadak atau dalam jumlah yang tidak sesuai kebutuhan operasional harian.
Evaluasi kinerja operasional menjadi semakin penting karena tingkat kepuasan
pelanggan sangat dipengaruhi oleh pengalaman layanan yang mereka rasakan secara
langsung. Pelanggan menuntut layanan yang cepat, kualitas potong rambut yang konsisten,
kenyamanan ruang layanan, serta ketepatan waktu penyelesaian. Dalam kondisi
persaingan yang ketat, kegagalan dalam memenuhi ekspektasi tersebut dapat mendorong
pelanggan untuk beralih ke barbershop lain yang menawarkan nilai layanan lebih baik.
Dengan demikian, penelitian ini dianggap penting untuk memberikan pemahaman
komprehensif mengenai kondisi kinerja operasional barbershop, mengidentifikasi
permasalahan yang muncul, serta menawarkan solusi berbasis pendekatan SCM dan
manajemen produksi untuk meningkatkan efektivitas operasional secara menyeluruh.
Temuan penelitian diharapkan dapat mendukung pemilik barbershop dalam mengambil
keputusan strategis untuk meningkatkan kualitas layanan, efisiensi operasional, serta
keberlanjutan bisnis di masa mendatang.
Metodologi
1. Desain Penelitian
Penelitian ini menggunakan pendekatan deskriptif kualitatif yang diperkaya dengan
data kuantitatif. Pemilihan desain ini bertujuan untuk memperoleh pemahaman
komprehensif mengenai kinerja operasional barbershop melalui integrasi konsep Supply
Chain Management (SCM) dan Manajemen Produksi. Pendekatan kualitatif digunakan untuk
menganalisis proses layanan, perilaku operasional, serta persepsi para pelaku usaha.
Sementara itu, data kuantitatif dimanfaatkan untuk memperkuat analisis melalui
pengukuran waktu layanan, tingkat ketersediaan persediaan, serta berbagai indikator
operasi lainnya.
https://penerbitadm.pubmedia.id/index.php/JER

Jurnal Ekonomi, Manajemen, Bisnis dan Akuntansi Review Vol: 5, No 2, 2025 4 of 10
2. Lokasi dan Waktu Penelitian
Penelitian dilaksanakan di Barber Shop LIDYA, yang beralamat di Jl. Dewi Sartika,
Kota Gorontalo. Observasi lapangan dilakukan sepanjang jam operasional (10.00–20.00)
dalam satu hari kerja penuh untuk menangkap dinamika operasional baik pada periode
ramai maupun sepi.
3. Teknik Pengumpulan Data
a. Observasi Non-Partisipan
Peneliti melakukan pengamatan langsung terhadap aktivitas layanan tanpa terlibat dalam
proses kerja. Aspek yang diamati meliputi:
1. pola kedatangan pelanggan
2. durasi total layanan menggunakan teknik time-motion study
3. jumlah dan panjang antrian
4. alur proses pelayanan
5. kondisi dan ketersediaan persediaan
Instrumen yang digunakan berupa lembar observasi yang mencatat arrival time, start
service, dan end service.
b. Wawancara Semi-Terstruktur
Wawancara dilakukan dengan pemilik barbershop dan dua barber untuk memperoleh
pemahaman mendalam mengenai:
1. mekanisme pengadaan bahan
2. jenis pemasok dan waktu pemesanan (lead time)
3. prosedur layanan
4. sistem penjadwalan barber
5. kendala operasional
Model semi-terstruktur dipilih agar peneliti dapat mengeksplorasi informasi secara
mendalam namun tetap sesuai fokus penelitian.
c. Audit Persediaan
Audit persediaan dilakukan dengan memeriksa:
1. jumlah stok awal
2. stok masuk dan keluar
3. frekuensi pembelian
4. pola penggunaan bahan habis pakai
Data audit ini digunakan untuk menganalisis tingkat ketersediaan barang dan
mengidentifikasi potensi stock-out.
d. Kuesioner Singkat
Kuesioner diberikan kepada pelanggan untuk mengukur persepsi mengenai:
1. kualitas layanan
2. lama waktu tunggu
3. kenyamanan tempat
4. konsistensi hasil layanan
Penilaian dilakukan menggunakan skala Likert 1–5.
https://penerbitadm.pubmedia.id/index.php/JER

Jurnal Ekonomi, Manajemen, Bisnis dan Akuntansi Review Vol: 5, No 2, 2025 5 of 10
4. Teknik Analisis Data
a. Analisis Kualitatif
Proses analisis kualitatif mengikuti tahapan menurut Miles & Huberman, yaitu:
1. Reduksi data — menyeleksi dan menyederhanakan informasi yang relevan dengan
SCM dan manajemen produksi.
2. Penyajian data — memvisualisasikan informasi mengenai alur layanan, aktivitas
barber, dan kondisi rantai pasok.
3. Penarikan kesimpulan — mengidentifikasi permasalahan inti serta merumuskan
alternatif perbaikan operasional.
b. Analisis Kuantitatif Operasional
Beberapa teknik analisis kuantitatif yang digunakan meliputi:
• Time-Motion Analysis, untuk menghitung rata-rata durasi layanan, variasi waktu, dan
identifikasi bottleneck.
• Little’s Law untuk menganalisis tingkat antrian dan rata-rata waktu tunggu pelanggan.
𝐿 = 𝜆𝑊
• Economic Order Quantity (EOQ) dalam menentukan jumlah pemesanan ideal agar
biaya persediaan lebih efisien.
• Evaluasi Key Performance Indicators (KPI), meliputi:
a. tingkat ketersediaan stok (fill rate)
b. efisiensi biaya operasional
c. tingkat utilisasi barber
d. lead time pengadaan bahan
Penggabungan analisis kualitatif dan kuantitatif memungkinkan pemahaman yang
lebih objektif terhadap kinerja operasional barbershop.
5. Instrumen Penelitian
Instrumen yang digunakan mencakup:
1. lembar observasi
2. pedoman wawancara
3. formulir audit persediaan
4. kuesioner pelanggan
5. stopwatch untuk pencatatan waktu
6. catatan lapangan (field notes)
Pengembangan instrumen mengacu pada teori SCM (Chopra & Meindl, 2016) serta
manajemen operasi (Heizer et al., 2017; Stevenson, 2018).
6. Validitas dan Reliabilitas Data
Untuk menjamin kredibilitas data, penelitian menerapkan:
a. Triangulasi sumber, yaitu membandingkan hasil observasi, wawancara, kuesioner, dan
data dokumen.
b. Triangulasi metode, dengan menggabungkan wawancara, observasi, dan audit
persediaan.
c. Pengecekan ulang (cross-check) kepada pemilik untuk memastikan bahwa data dan
temuan sesuai dengan kondisi operasional sebenarnya.
https://penerbitadm.pubmedia.id/index.php/JER

Jurnal Ekonomi, Manajemen, Bisnis dan Akuntansi Review Vol: 5, No 2, 2025 6 of 10
7. Batasan Penelitian
Adapun batasan penelitian ini antara lain:
a. Pengamatan hanya dilakukan dalam satu hari, sehingga tidak mencerminkan fluktuasi
permintaan jangka panjang.
b. Data biaya dan operasional diperoleh terbatas pada informasi yang bersedia diberikan
oleh pemilik.
c. Fokus penelitian hanya pada satu lokasi sehingga hasilnya tidak dapat digeneralisasikan
secara luas.
Hasil dan Pembahasan
1. Operasional Barber Shop LIDYA
Hasil observasi menunjukkan bahwa Barber Shop LIDYA beroperasi dengan tiga
barber aktif dan melayani pelanggan menggunakan sistem walk-in serta konfirmasi melalui
telepon. Alur layanan terdiri dari kedatangan pelanggan, registrasi manual, konsultasi,
proses pemotongan, finishing, dan pembayaran. Waktu layanan yang dicatat selama
penelitian berkisar antara 20–45 menit tergantung kompleksitas permintaan pelanggan.
Variasi durasi layanan tersebut dipengaruhi oleh keterampilan barber, kelengkapan
persediaan, serta kondisi antrean pada jam sibuk. Situasi ini menunjukkan adanya
ketidakkonsistenan dalam standar proses, yang berdampak pada pengalaman pelanggan.
2. Evaluasi Rantai Pasok (Supply Chain Management)
Manajemen Pemasok
Barber Shop LIDYA bergantung pada satu pemasok utama untuk produk styling dan
dua pemasok alternatif untuk neck tissue dan sanitizer. Pola pemesanan bersifat mingguan
untuk bahan habis pakai dan bulanan untuk produk styling.
Permasalahan utama yang ditemukan:
• Lead time bervariasi antara 2–7 hari, sehingga ketersediaan barang tidak selalu terjamin.
• Ketergantungan pada satu pemasok utama meningkatkan risiko ketika terjadi
keterlambatan pengiriman.
Menurut Chopra & Meindl (2016), ketergantungan pada satu pemasok meningkatkan
risiko supply uncertainty, terutama apabila variasi lead time tidak dapat diprediksi. Hal ini
terbukti dalam penelitian ketika terjadi keterlambatan pasokan yang menyebabkan
penggantian bahan dan memperpanjang waktu layanan.
Pengendalian Persediaan
Pencatatan stok dilakukan secara manual melalui buku kas kecil dan tidak didukung
sistem inventori. Pada hari ke-3 observasi ditemukan kejadian stock-out neck tissue
selama satu hari. Akibatnya, barber harus mengganti bahan dengan handuk sekali pakai,
yang kemudian memperlambat proses layanan.
https://penerbitadm.pubmedia.id/index.php/JER

Jurnal Ekonomi, Manajemen, Bisnis dan Akuntansi Review Vol: 5, No 2, 2025 7 of 10
Dampak operasional:
• Waktu layanan meningkat karena pergantian bahan tidak efisien.
• Pembelian darurat menyebabkan kenaikan biaya bahan hingga 25% lebih tinggi
dibanding pembelian reguler.
• Risiko downtime meningkat bila terjadi stock-out pada bahan kritis lain seperti alkohol
atau sanitizer.
Menurut teori Inventory Management, khususnya Economic Order Quantity (EOQ)
(Stevenson, 2018), ketidakteraturan dalam pola pemesanan menyebabkan biaya operasional
meningkat dan risiko stock-out lebih besar. Kondisi barber shop menunjukkan rendahnya
nilai fill rate (88%), yang berarti 12% permintaan layanan terganggu akibat
ketidaktersediaan bahan. Angka ini menunjukkan bahwa kontrol persediaan belum efektif.
3. Analisis Proses Layanan (Production Management)
Time-Motion Study
Pengamatan terhadap 50 pelanggan menunjukkan adanya variasi waktu layanan
yang cukup besar (standar deviasi tinggi). Variasi ini mencerminkan bahwa tidak terdapat
SOP yang diterapkan secara konsisten oleh seluruh barber.
Rata-rata waktu layanan:
• Potong cepat: 20 menit
• Potong + styling: 45 menit
Variabilitas layanan tanpa SOP jelas merupakan penyebab utama ketidakkonsistenan
proses. Heizer & Munson (2020) menjelaskan bahwa variabilitas tinggi akan
memperpanjang waktu tunggu dan mengurangi produktivitas, karena proses menjadi lebih
sulit diprediksi.
Sistem Antrian dan Waktu Tunggu
Pada jam sibuk (13.00–15.00), waktu tunggu rata-rata mencapai 30–45 menit,
sementara pada jam sepi hanya 5–10 menit. Melalui pendekatan Little’s Law, tingginya
kedatangan pelanggan pada jam tertentu menyebabkan antrian menumpuk karena
kapasitas barber tidak seimbang dengan demand.
Dampak:
• Beberapa pelanggan memilih meninggalkan barbershop.
• Tingkat kepuasan pelanggan menurun pada jam puncak.
Utilisasi Barber
Dengan rata-rata 36 pelanggan per hari dan waktu layanan 30 menit per pelanggan,
utilisasi barber mendekati 70–85% pada jam sibuk, menandakan beban kerja tinggi.
Kondisi ini dapat menurunkan kualitas layanan karena barber kelelahan.
Menurut teori produksi, utilisasi mendekati 100% akan meningkatkan kesalahan kerja,
menurunkan kualitas, dan memperpanjang waktu layanan (Heizer & Render, 2020).
Kondisi ini menjadi penjelasan mengapa durasi layanan pada jam sibuk semakin panjang
dan hasil potongan rambut kurang konsisten.

4. Indikator Kinerja Operasional (KPI)
Berikut ringkasan temuan KPI:
Indikator Hasil Temuan Implikasi

5. Pembahasan Temuan Utama
Integrasi SCM dan Manajemen Produksi
Hasil penelitian menunjukkan bahwa sebagian besar permasalahan operasional muncul
karena:
• ketiadaan sistem pengendalian persediaan,
• SOP layanan yang belum distandardisasi,
• penjadwalan barber yang tidak optimal.
Ketiga aspek tersebut saling berhubungan. Keterlambatan pasokan menyebabkan
perubahan alur layanan, SOP yang tidak jelas mengakibatkan ketidakseragaman output,
dan penjadwalan barber yang tidak terstruktur menimbulkan antrian panjang.
Dampak terhadap Pengalaman Pelanggan
Variasi waktu layanan dan antrian yang panjang menurunkan persepsi pelanggan
terhadap profesionalisme barbershop. Sesuai teori service operations, konsistensi layanan
adalah faktor kritis dalam industri jasa (Stevenson, 2018).
Potensi Perbaikan
Untuk meningkatkan kinerja operasional, barbershop dapat:
• menerapkan sistem inventori sederhana (misalnya spreadsheet),
• membuat SOP layanan standar,
• menerapkan sistem booking untuk mengurangi ketidakpastian antrian,
• menetapkan safety stock untuk bahan penting,
• menilai pemasok secara berkala dan menambah mitra pemasok.
Simpulan
Penelitian ini menunjukkan bahwa kinerja operasional Barber Shop LIDYA sangat
dipengaruhi oleh efektivitas pengelolaan rantai pasok dan proses produksi layanan.
Temuan observasi mengindikasikan bahwa ketiadaan sistem pengendalian persediaan
yang terorganisasi, ketergantungan pada pemasok tertentu, serta ketidakseragaman
standar operasional menjadi faktor utama yang memicu rendahnya efisiensi layanan.
Kondisi tersebut berkontribusi pada meningkatnya waktu tunggu pelanggan, tingginya
https://penerbitadm.pubmedia.id/index.php/JER

Jurnal Ekonomi, Manajemen, Bisnis dan Akuntansi Review Vol: 5, No 2, 2025 9 of 10
variasi durasi pelayanan, serta munculnya kasus stock-out yang menghambat kelancaran
proses kerja barber.
Dari perspektif Supply Chain Management (SCM), Barber Shop LIDYA masih
menghadapi beberapa persoalan, seperti belum optimalnya pengendalian stok,
ketidakteraturan jadwal pemesanan, dan fluktuasi lead time pemasok. Sementara itu,
ditinjau dari sisi Manajemen Produksi, proses layanan belum didukung dengan SOP yang
distandardisasi sehingga kualitas hasil layanan antar barber belum konsisten. Selain itu,
kapasitas layanan belum dimanfaatkan secara efisien akibat absennya mekanisme
penjadwalan yang mampu mengatur arus pelanggan, terutama pada periode permintaan
tinggi.
Secara keseluruhan, hasil penelitian menegaskan bahwa integrasi konsep SCM dan
Manajemen Produksi berperan penting dalam meningkatkan efisiensi operasional
barbershop. Penerapan kedua pendekatan tersebut berpotensi memperbaiki kecepatan dan
konsistensi layanan, meminimalkan risiko kekurangan persediaan, mengurangi
pemborosan biaya, serta meningkatkan kepuasan pelanggan. Melalui pengelolaan
inventori yang lebih sistematis, penerapan SOP yang seragam, dan evaluasi kinerja secara
berkala, Barber Shop LIDYA memiliki peluang untuk memperkuat daya saing dan
meningkatkan kualitas layanan secara berkelanjutan.

text:
Journal of Economics and Business UBS e-ISSN: 2774-7042 p-ISSN: 2302-8025
Vol. 12 No. 4 Juli-Agustus 2023
PROPOSED BUSINESS STRATEGY FOR SUSTAINABLE BARBERSHOP
PARTNERSHIP MODEL (CASE STUDY: KOLEGA BARBER)
Galih Ikhsan Fadillah1, Hasnul Suhaimi2 Yos Sunitiyoso3
Institut Teknologi Bandung
Email: galih_fadillah@sbm-itb.ac.id, hasnuls@gmail.com yos.sunitiyoso@sbm-itb.ac.id
Abstract
The barbershop industry in Indonesia has seen significant growth in recent years,
with an annual growth rate of 20-30%. Kolega Barber is one of the players in this
industry, utilizing a partnership model to target entrepreneurs and foster
collaboration among stakeholders. However, the lack of standardization in the
interior of the barbershops poses a significant challenge, making it difficult for
potential partners to know what to expect. This study aims to analyze the current
business situation of Kolega Barber's partnership model and develop a sustainable
partnership model by addressing challenges identified through a 7Ps analysis. The
study employs qualitative research methods, including interviews with Kolega
partners and observation. PESTEL, Porter five forces, VRIO, and SWOT
frameworks are used for analysis, and a business model canvas is developed using
the TOWS Matrix. The strategic implementation plan focuses on SOP
development, human capital development, monitoring and controlling programs,
and promotional support. Kolega Barber's long-term success and customer loyalty
depend on building strong partnerships, leveraging technology, and emphasizing
sustainability and a unique store concept.
Keywords: Barbershop, Sustainable, Partnership, Business Strategy, Franchise.
Introduction
The barbershop industry in Indonesia has shown promising growth of 20-30%
every year, with men being 44% more likely to have their hair cut monthly than women
(Plumb, 2015). The market for barbershops in Indonesia remains highly attractive, with
5,000 barbershop brands throughout the country, including Kolega Barbershop.
Initially, Kolega Barbershop only offered basic shaving services. To accelerate growth,
they developed a partnership system that offers barbershop partnership packages to the
public (Welch, 2008). This approach promotes collaboration between entrepreneurs,
barbers, and related industries, creating a sustainable ecosystem for the barbershop
industry. However, the effectiveness of the current partnership system is questionable,
and Kolega Barbershop faces several management issues, including late payment of
royalty fees and a lack of consistent business support for partners. Control and
monitoring are essential to creating a sustainable partnership model, and the company
must focus on central management to address these issues (Cherrafi et al., 2017). The
partnership agreement includes clauses related to managing employees, salaries, and an
unclear business partner profit percentage. To create a sustainable partnership model,
Kolega Barbershop needs to improve its partner management, with a focus on control
and monitoring, and consistent business support (Holjevac et al., 2022). The aim of this
2249

Galih Ikhsan Fadillah1, Hasnul Suhaimi2 dan Yos Sunitiyoso3
research is to analyze the current business situation of Kolega Barber's partnership
model and to develop a new business strategy that will create a sustainable partnership
model. The scope and limitations of this research are as follows: firstly, the research
will focus solely on developing a partnership strategy for Kolega Barber. Secondly, the
research will not cover any data outside of Kolega Barber. Thirdly, the survey
respondents for this research will only be Kolega Barber's partners. Lastly, the
information used in this study will be obtained through focus group discussions and
interviews to ensure that the data collected is accurate and reliable (Taherdoost, 2021).
Effective franchising involves corporate sustainability, encompassing economic,
environmental, and social aspects (Clarkin & Swavely, 2006). However, few franchise
firms, especially those owned by local franchisors, have managed to survive in their
respective industries for more than 10 years due to a lack of cash-based business capital
(Samsudin & Wahab, 2018). (Jang & Park, 2019) propose a new model for achieving a
sustainable and mutually beneficial relationship between franchisors and franchisees, a
crucial aspect of the franchise win-win theory. The quality of the relationship between
these two parties is widely considered to be the most crucial element in ensuring the
longevity and prosperity of a franchise system (Karchegani, Sofian, & Amin, 2013). To
measure the level of relationship quality more objectively, the study draws upon the
commonly utilized components of satisfaction, trust, and commitment from previous
relationship marketing studies. The relationship between the franchisor and the
franchisee is a commercial partnership that facilitates cooperation within the franchise
business. Relationships based on opportunistic conduct can erode trust and strain the
relationship in the future (Glavee-Geo, Burki, & Buvik, 2020) The development of a
sustainable partnership model is an effective way for firms to collaborate with other
businesses and stakeholders towards long-term success. In the context of the men's
grooming or barbershop industry, a sustainable partnership model can enable the
formation of partnerships that benefit all parties, including customers, businesses, and
other stakeholders (Kim, Lee, Lee, & Lee, 2021).
Method
A case study involves the empirical investigation of contemporary phenomena
using multiple sources of evidence (Barnes, 2001). A case study is distinct from an
experimental strategy, which is not bound to a particular context. It is most suitable for
gaining in-depth insight into the research context. The author used a qualitative research
method with a case study approach. Qualitative research begins by exploring a
particular area, collecting data, and generating ideas and hypotheses from this data,
mostly through what is known as inductive reasoning. Data collection can be carried out
in various settings and using different sources and methods. In this study, the
researchers employed interviews and observation as data collection methods. The
interview is one of the most commonly used tools for collecting qualitative research
data. It allows researchers to collect data from a variety of respondents in different
situations and contexts (Sutton & Austin, 2015). Data analysis is the process of
2250

Business Strategy for Sustainable Barbershop Partnership
(Case Study: Kolega Barber)
systematically searching for and compiling data obtained from interviews, field notes,
and documentation by organizing the data into categories, describing them as units,
synthesizing them, compiling them into patterns, choosing which ones are important
and which ones will be learned, and drawing conclusions so that they are easily
understood by oneself and others.
To create a sustainable business partnership model for Kolega Barber, several
gaps need to be addressed based on the 7Ps analysis. The feedback obtained from the
partner data collection was then used to develop a strategic implementation plan to
achieve a sustainable partnership model. The implementation plan focuses on
addressing the identified problems and enhancing the business environment of the
barbershop industry. Additionally, the feedback serves as the basis for formulating
strategies to improve the core competencies of Kolega Barber. To develop a sustainable
partnership model, the authors utilized the Development of a Business Model Canvas to
create a model for Kolega Barber. Interviews with partners were also conducted to
ensure that the business solution was comprehensive and effective.
Result and Discussion
To ensure that the business solution was comprehensive and effective, interviews
with partners were conducted to obtain their insights and feedback on the existing
business model canvas. This helped in identifying areas requiring development and
generating potential solutions. Partner feedback was then used to refine the business
model canvas and develop a partnership model for Kolega Barber that is more
sustainable.
Figure 2
7P’s Root-Cause Analysis

Galih Ikhsan Fadillah1, Hasnul Suhaimi2 dan Yos Sunitiyoso3
Key gaps in Kolega Barber's business partnership model, based on the 7Ps
analysis, and their corresponding solutions are:
1. Place: Invest in a centralized warehouse to streamline operations and ensure
adequate inventory management and logistics.
2. Physical Evidence: Standardize the interior design of all barber rooms and
develop unique selling points to increase brand recognition.
3. Promotion: Hire a dedicated marketing team to manage social media, run paid
advertising campaigns, and collaborate with influencers to increase brand
awareness.
4. Process: Invest in a robust human resource management system to reduce
turnover rates and attract skilled barbers, and establish consistent delivery
systems for supplies and equipment.
5. Price: Conduct market research to determine the optimal pricing strategy that
balances affordability and profitability, and consider offering promotions or
discounts to attract and retain customers.
6. People: Invest in negotiation training programs for partners and establish
strong human resource management practices to reduce turnover rates and
improve service quality.
7. Product: Develop a loyalty program or rewards system for repeat customers,
build a strong brand image through effective marketing, and expand product
offerings to attract a wider range of customers.
To obtain qualitative data from ten Kolega Barber partners, a comprehensive
research methodology was used. The purpose of the study was to investigate the
perspectives of fairness, autonomy, formalization, support, and satisfaction in order to
establish trust and commitment as part of the Kolega Barber sustainable partnership
model. In conclusion, the partnership model that Kolega Barber implements has
received positive feedback from its partners. They like the fact that the business is
dedicated to supplying its partners with barbers of excellent quality and offering them
thorough support.
Table 1 Interview Key Take Away
1. What is your opinion on the partnership system at Kolega Barber
that you have experienced so far?
Kolega Barber has created a successful partnership model with satisfied
partners who have praised the brand's management standards, ongoing
support, and efforts to enhance the quality of barbers.
2. What are your reasons for joining into a partnership with Kolega
Barber?
Kolega Barbershop Partnership's success can be attributed to its
commitment to providing high-quality grooming services and support to its

partners can expect to receive guidance and assistance throughout their
